metadata.notes: agy HAS a !cmd shell-escape (typing ! activates bash mode; the command runs locally with NO transcript line — live-verified 2026-06-20: !echo hello → ⎿ hello, zero transcript.jsonl created). State stays ready through the escape; the follow-up prompt mints the conv and proves liveness. keys + wait_turn ported; recorded live.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

**agent=yes.** agy ships a `!cmd` shell-escape — the binary string `"Start your message with ! to run a shell command."` is the in-REPL affordance, mirroring claudecode's `!echo hello`. The shell runs locally with no model round trip.

**daemon=full (provisional).** The scenario's intent reading (adopted by the claudecode sibling) is 'state must not stick in working — no synthesized turn'. The antigravity parser only mints state from `USER_INPUT` (working), `PLANNER_RESPONSE` (assistant/turn_done) and MODEL tool-result lines; it has no path that would classify a user-initiated shell escape as a turn. So if the shell escape is absent from the filtered transcript.jsonl (the expected case — it is a TUI-local shell, not a MODEL tool_call), state stays `ready` throughout, which satisfies the scenario. The follow-up normal prompt then drives a clean `ready → working → ready` arc, proving the session is alive. CAVEAT (emission, unconfirmed): no sampled transcript contains a `!cmd` escape, so whether agy writes ANY wrapper line for it to the FILTERED transcript is unverified. If it writes a `USER_INPUT`-shaped line, the parser would (wrongly) open a turn — that would be a `daemon=bug`. `record` must confirm which: this is exactly the emission question the SKILL says only a live recording can settle.

**driver=gap:wait_turn.** The recipe needs `keys` (to type the literal `!echo hello`, since a `send` of `!...` plus Enter would still need raw keystrokes for the bang prefix) AND `wait_turn` (the final-turn settle). Both are `not_implemented` stubs in the scaffolded driver (`DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"`). `wait_turn` is the load-bearing gap; `keys` is also unported. `record` ports both before driving.
caveats: Driver needs BOTH keys (bang-prefixed keystroke) and wait_turn ported; the gap primitive recorded is wait_turn (the turn-settle), keys is the secondary.
Tokens/cost not in JSONL — observations assert cost/tokens = 0.

CONFIRMED (live record 2026-06-20): the !cmd shell-escape is fully TUI-local — !echo hello produced ⎿ hello and created NO transcript.jsonl at all. The parser never sees it, so state stays ready; daemon=full holds (not a bug). The follow-up prompt mints the conv and arcs ready→working→ready.

sources.note: strings: 'Start your message with ! to run a shell command.' — confirms the !cmd shell-escape affordance (v1.0.10)
no parser path classifies a user shell-escape as a turn; only USER_INPUT / PLANNER_RESPONSE / MODEL tool-result lines drive state
keys and wait_turn arms are not_implemented; DRIVE_ELICITS lacks both

preconditions: agy CLI >=1.0.10 on PATH, authenticated via Google OAuth
setup: Fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/; agy mints its own <conv-id>

script.keys: ! e c h o Space h e l l o Enter

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: alive

verify: during the ~5s window after !echo hello, NO state_transition to working or waiting for the session (shell escape is not a turn)
after the normal prompt, events.jsonl shows ready→working→ready
